title: humboltia obovata
intro: Humboldtia obovata, also known as the "holy tree" or "kayu putih", is a species of tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Humboldtia obovata include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Humboldtia obovata can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
